---
title: "journal reading PCI STEMI DCB Eng"
author: "謝慕揚 MD, PhD, FESC"
date: 2026-03-27
lang: zh-TW
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
number-sections: true
---

\maketitle


## Introduction

This literature review summarizes studies on Drug-Coated Balloon (DCB) treatment in STEMI patients, including case series and randomized controlled trials (RCTs).


## Case Series Studies


| p{2.5cm}p{3cm}p{6cm}} Summary of Case Series Studies on DCB Treatment for STEMI |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Study/Author** | **Year/Sample Size** | **Follow-up** |
| [Vukadinović et al.](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ccd.31699) | 2025 / Single case | 7 weeks |
| [Multicenter case series](https://www.mdpi.com/2077-0383/14/3/869) | 2025 / 118 cases | Median 23.2 months |
| [Vos et al.](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK543588/) | 2018 / 100 cases | 1 year |


## Randomized Controlled Trials


| p{2cm}p{2.5cm}p{2cm}p{5.5cm}} Summary of RCTs on DCB Treatment for STEMI |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Trial Name** | **Year/Sample** | **Study Design** |
| [REVELATION](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31126887/) | 2019 / N unclear | Prospective, randomized, single-center     DCB vs. DES |
| [Chinese RCT](https://cardiothoracicsurgery.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s13019-021-01525-8) | 2021 / 80 cases | Randomized     DCB (n=38)     vs.     DES (n=42) |
| [REC-CAGEFREE I](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0140673624015940) | 2024 / Large scale | Open-label, randomized, non-inferiority trial     43 centers (China)     DCB+bailout stenting     vs.     Planned DES |


## Meta-analyses and Systematic Reviews


| p{2cm}p{3cm}p{6cm}} Meta-analyses on DCB for AMI/STEMI |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Study Type** | **Year** | **Studies/Patients Included** |
| [DCB vs. DES in AMI meta-analysis](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10406147/) | 2023 | Search period:     April 2002 - Oct 2022     RCTs and observational studies |
| [Large vessel de novo lesion meta-analysis](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-025-85734-4) | 2025 | 15 studies     3,975 patients     (2,114 DCB) |


## Advantages and Limitations of DCB in STEMI


### Advantages


1. Avoids permanent metallic implants, reducing late stent thrombosis risk
2. Preserves vasomotor function, maintains vessel contractile response and geometry
3. Promotes positive vessel remodeling (as shown in Chinese RCT: negative LLL)
4. Allows shorter dual antiplatelet therapy duration, beneficial for high bleeding risk patients
5. Reduces in-stent restenosis without blind spots from metal struts affecting drug delivery
6. Particularly attractive for young patients, avoiding lifelong metallic implants


### Limitations and Challenges


1. Most evidence from case series or small-scale RCTs
2. REC-CAGEFREE I did not meet non-inferiority criteria
3. Requires meticulous lesion preparation techniques (pre-dilation, dissection assessment)
4. Approximately 3-40
5. Lack of long-term follow-up data (>3 years)
6. Higher technical demands on operators
7. Not suitable for severely calcified lesions


## Conclusion

Current evidence for DCB use in STEMI primarily comes from case series and small-scale RCTs. The REVELATION trial provides the most important RCT evidence, demonstrating the safety and feasibility of DCB in STEMI, with non-inferiority to DES in FFR assessment. Despite encouraging results, **large-scale, multicenter randomized clinical trials are still needed** to further establish the role of DCB-PCI in STEMI patients.


## Future Research Directions


1. Large-scale multicenter RCTs (target enrollment >500 patients)
2. Long-term follow-up studies (5-10 years)
3. Subgroup analyses (young patients, diabetes, high bleeding risk)
4. Direct comparison of DCB with latest-generation DES
5. Imaging-guided DCB strategies (OCT/IVUS)
6. Standardization of optimal lesion preparation techniques


## References


1. Vukadinović D, et al. [Drug Coated Balloon in Young Patients With ST-Elevation Myocardial Infarction: A Viable Treatment Option?](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ccd.31699) *Catheterization and Cardiovascular Interventions*. 2025.
2. [Culprit-Lesion Drug-Coated-Balloon Percutaneous Coronary Intervention in Patients Presenting with ST-Elevation Myocardial Infarction (STEMI).](https://www.mdpi.com/2077-0383/14/3/869) *J Clin Med*. 2025;14(3):869.
3. Vos NS, et al. [Drug-Coated Balloons in STEMI - Primary Angioplasty.](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK543588/) *NCBI Bookshelf*. 2018.
4. Nijhoff F, et al. [Paclitaxel-Coated Balloon Angioplasty Versus Drug-Eluting Stent in Acute Myocardial Infarction: The REVELATION Randomized Trial.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31126887/) *JACC Cardiovasc Interv*. 2019;12(17):1691-1699.
5. [Study on the safety and effectiveness of drug-coated balloons in patients with acute myocardial infarction.](https://cardiothoracicsurgery.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s13019-021-01525-8) *J Cardiothorac Surg*. 2021;16:244.
6. [Drug-Coated Balloon Angioplasty With Rescue Stenting Versus Intended Stenting for the Treatment of Patients With De Novo Coronary Artery Lesions (REC-CAGEFREE I): An Open-Label, Randomised, Non-Inferiority Trial.](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0140673624015940) *The Lancet*. 2024;404(10457):1040-1050.
7. [Drug-Coated Balloon vs. Drug-Eluting Stent in Acute Myocardial Infarction: A Systematic Review and Updated Meta-Analysis.](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10406147/) *PMC*. 2023.
8. [Drug coated balloon angioplasty for de novo coronary lesions in large vessels: a systematic review and meta-analysis.](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-025-85734-4) *Sci Rep*. 2025;15:2734.
9. [Drug-Coated Balloon in Primary Percutaneous Coronary Intervention.](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10317576/) *PMC*. 2023.
